title: Global Markets React as US and Iran Reach Framework Peace Deal
subtitle: While the agreement offers hope for lower energy prices, experts warn that a full return to normal shipping will take time.

summary.en: The United States and Iran have reached a framework agreement to end their recent conflict. This deal aims to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, a vital route for global oil and gas shipments. While oil prices have dropped following the announcement, experts caution that shipping will not return to normal immediately. Many vessels remain stuck in the region, and clearing the waterway of mines is a complex task. Global markets are watching closely to see if this agreement leads to a lasting peace.
summary.zh: 美國與伊朗已達成一項結束近期衝突的框架協議。該協議旨在重啟全球石油與天然氣運輸的關鍵路徑——荷莫茲海峽。儘管消息公布後油價下跌，但專家警告航運不會立即恢復正常。許多船隻仍受困於該地區，且清除水道中的水雷是一項複雜的任務。全球市場正密切關注這項協議是否能帶來持久的和平。

paragraphs.en: After more than three months of conflict, the United States and Iran have announced a framework agreement to end hostilities. This development has brought a sense of relief to global energy markets, which have been under significant pressure since the crisis began in late February. The conflict effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway that serves as a critical path for about one-fifth of the world’s oil and natural gas supplies. The closure caused global energy prices to rise sharply, affecting the cost of fuel for businesses and families around the world.
paragraphs.zh: 在經歷超過三個月的衝突後，美國與伊朗宣布達成一項結束敵對狀態的框架協議。這項進展為全球能源市場帶來了緩解的氣息，自二月底危機爆發以來，能源市場一直承受著巨大壓力。這場衝突實質上封鎖了荷莫茲海峽，這條狹窄的水道是全球約五分之一石油與天然氣供應的關鍵路徑。封鎖導致全球能源價格急劇飆升，影響了世界各地企業與家庭的燃料成本。

paragraphs.en: Following the news of the agreement, US President Donald Trump expressed optimism, stating on social media that ships should prepare to move and that the strait would soon be safe for commercial travel. Market data shows that the price of Brent crude, the global oil benchmark, fell to around $83.55 a barrel after the announcement. This is a notable decrease from the peak of $120 a barrel reached during the height of the fighting, though it remains higher than the average price recorded last year, according to reports from The Guardian and BBC Business.
paragraphs.zh: 在協議消息傳出後，美國總統川普（Donald Trump）表達了樂觀態度，他在社群媒體上表示，船隻應準備啟航，且該海峽很快就能恢復商業航行的安全。市場數據顯示，作為全球石油基準的布蘭特原油價格在消息公布後，跌至每桶約八十三點五五美元。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）與英國廣播公司商業新聞（BBC Business）的報導，儘管這仍高於去年的平均價格，但已較衝突高峰期每桶一百二十美元的價格顯著下降。

paragraphs.en: Despite the positive news, industry experts warn that the road to normality will be long. Neil Shearing, the group chief economist for Capital Economics, noted that it remains unclear whether this deal is a temporary truce or a permanent settlement. Even with a signed agreement, significant logistical challenges remain. Many oil tankers are currently in the wrong locations, and production facilities need time to return to full capacity. Furthermore, there are ongoing concerns regarding the cost and availability of insurance for ships that must travel through the area.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管消息正面，產業專家警告恢復正常之路將會漫長。凱投宏觀（Capital Economics）的集團首席經濟學家尼爾·希林（Neil Shearing）指出，目前尚不清楚這項協議是暫時的停火，還是永久的解決方案。即便簽署了協議，仍存在重大的物流挑戰。許多油輪目前位置錯誤，且生產設施需要時間才能恢復全產能。此外，對於必須通過該地區船隻的保險成本與取得管道，各界仍持續感到擔憂。

paragraphs.en: Shipping companies are also approaching the situation with caution. For example, the shipping giant Maersk reported that it still has five ships stuck in the Gulf and that it is too early to change its current operations. Similarly, Hapag-Lloyd, another major shipping firm, stated it hopes to move its vessels once the deal is finalized and any remaining sea mines are cleared. According to data from Lloyd’s List, more than 160 vessels have been unable to leave the region for over 100 days. Clearing these mines is expected to be a slow process that could take several weeks.
paragraphs.zh: 航運公司對此局勢也採取謹慎態度。例如，航運巨頭馬士基（Maersk）表示，仍有五艘船隻受困於波斯灣，現在改變當前營運模式還為時過早。同樣地，另一家大型航運公司赫伯羅特（Hapag-Lloyd）也表示，希望在協議最終敲定且剩餘的水雷清除後，再調動其船隻。根據勞氏日報（Lloyd’s List）的數據，已有超過一百六十艘船隻在該地區受困超過一百天。清除這些水雷預計將是一個緩慢的過程，可能需要數週時間。

paragraphs.en: Analysts from Deutsche Welle (DW) highlighted that the global economy avoided the worst-case scenarios largely because of "demand destruction." This means that as prices rose, countries like China significantly reduced their oil imports and relied on their own domestic stockpiles instead. While this helped keep prices from reaching even higher levels, it also meant that global emergency reserves have been falling at an average rate of over five million barrels per day. Experts like Jorge Leon from Rystad Energy warned that these buffers are becoming very thin, making a successful reopening of the shipping route essential for future stability.
paragraphs.zh: 德國之聲（Deutsche Welle）的分析師強調，全球經濟之所以能避免最壞情況，很大程度上歸功於「需求破壞」。這意味著隨著價格上漲，中國等國家大幅減少了石油進口，轉而依賴國內庫存。雖然這有助於防止價格進一步飆升，但也意味著全球緊急儲備正以平均每天超過五百萬桶的速度下降。如雷斯塔能源（Rystad Energy）的豪爾赫·萊昂（Jorge Leon）等專家警告，這些緩衝資源正變得非常稀薄，因此成功重啟航運路線對於未來的穩定至關重要。

paragraphs.en: For the US, the timing of the deal is politically significant. With midterm elections approaching later this year, the Trump administration has faced pressure to lower fuel costs for American consumers. According to Bjarne Schieldrop, a chief commodities analyst at SEB, the administration will likely present this agreement as a major victory to help stabilize the economy before the voting season begins. Meanwhile, for Iran, a gradual reopening of the strait allows the government to maintain some political leverage during the ongoing 60-day negotiation period regarding its nuclear programs.
paragraphs.zh: 對美國而言，這項協議的時間點具有政治意義。隨著今年稍晚期中選舉即將到來，川普政府面臨降低美國消費者燃料成本的壓力。根據北歐斯安銀行（SEB）首席大宗商品分析師比亞恩·希爾德羅普（Bjarne Schieldrop）的說法，政府可能會將此協議視為一項重大勝利，以協助在投票季開始前穩定經濟。同時，對伊朗而言，海峽的逐步重啟使其政府能在針對核計畫為期六十天的談判期間，維持一定的政治籌碼。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, market observers believe that while some crude oil flows could resume by the end of the third quarter, the recovery for natural gas exports may take longer. This is partly due to damage caused by drone strikes on gas processing facilities in Qatar during the conflict. As the world waits for the formal signing of the agreement, the global economy remains in a delicate state. Whether the Strait of Hormuz can truly return to its role as a secure and busy trade route will depend on the continued cooperation between Washington and Tehran in the coming months.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，市場觀察家認為，雖然部分原油運輸可能在第三季末恢復，但天然氣出口的復甦可能需要更長時間。這部分歸因於衝突期間無人機襲擊卡達天然氣處理設施所造成的損害。在全世界等待協議正式簽署的同時，全球經濟仍處於脆弱狀態。荷莫茲海峽能否真正恢復其作為安全且繁忙貿易航道的角色，將取決於華盛頓與德黑蘭在未來幾個月內的持續合作。

keyTerms.definitionEn: Unable to move or be moved from a particular position.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作動詞 stick 的過去式，這裡作形容詞描述船隻無法移動的狀態。文中：For example, the shipping giant Maersk reported that it still has five ships stuck in the Gulf and that it is too early to change its current operations.

keyTerms.definitionEn: In this context, meaning low in quantity or supply.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指物體厚度薄，這裡指庫存量極低。文中：Experts like Jorge Leon from Rystad Energy warned that these buffers are becoming very thin, making a successful reopening of the shipping route essential for future stability.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Power or influence that can be used to achieve a desired result.
keyTerms.noteZh: 原指物理上的槓桿作用，這裡引申為政治談判中的籌碼。文中：Meanwhile, for Iran, a gradual reopening of the strait allows the government to maintain some political leverage during the ongoing 60-day negotiation period regarding its nuclear programs.

sources.name: The Guardian Business
sources.title: Oil and gas unlikely to return to prewar prices for months even if Hormuz reopens

sources.name: Deutsche Welle (DW) Top
sources.title: US-Iran deal: When will oil prices fall?

quiz.question: According to the article, what is the primary reason why natural gas exports might take longer to recover than crude oil?

options.A: The Strait of Hormuz is too shallow for natural gas tankers.
options.B: The global demand for natural gas has decreased significantly.
options.C: Drone strikes caused physical damage to gas processing facilities in Qatar.
options.D: Insurance companies refuse to cover natural gas shipments in the region.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章倒數第二段，文中明確提到天然氣出口恢復較慢的部分原因是卡達的天然氣處理設施在衝突期間遭到無人機襲擊而受損。選項 A 文中未提及；選項 B 與文中提到的「需求破壞」無關，且並非恢復慢的原因；選項 D 雖然提到保險問題，但這是針對所有船隻的普遍擔憂，而非天然氣出口恢復較慢的特定理由。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the global energy situation despite the new framework agreement?

options.A: The global economy has already fully recovered to pre-conflict levels.
options.B: Energy prices will immediately drop to the levels seen last year.
options.C: The stability of the energy market remains fragile due to depleted emergency reserves.
options.D: Shipping companies have already resumed normal operations in the Gulf.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據第五段，雖然「需求破壞」避免了最壞情況，但全球緊急儲備正以每天超過五百萬桶的速度減少，導致緩衝空間極度薄弱。這暗示了儘管有協議，市場仍處於脆弱狀態。選項 A 與文末提到的「delicate state」矛盾；選項 B 錯誤，因為文中提到價格仍高於去年平均；選項 D 不正確，因為 Maersk 等公司表示現在改變營運為時過早。

quiz.question: In the fifth paragraph, what does the phrase 'demand destruction' refer to?

options.A: The intentional sabotage of oil production facilities by warring nations.
options.B: A decrease in consumption caused by high prices and the use of stockpiles.
options.C: The complete collapse of the global energy market infrastructure.
options.D: The loss of oil reserves due to environmental disasters and spills.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據第五段，文中解釋「demand destruction」意指當價格上漲時，如中國等國家大幅減少石油進口並依賴國內庫存。這是一種經濟現象，而非實體破壞。選項 A 錯誤，因為這是指進口減少而非設施破壞；選項 C 過度誇大，文中並未發生市場基礎設施崩潰；選項 D 與文中對該詞的定義完全不符。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the article's main point?

options.A: The US and Iran have successfully resolved all political tensions through a new treaty.
options.B: Global energy markets are now completely stable following the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.
options.C: While an agreement has been reached, significant logistical and economic challenges to full recovery remain.
options.D: The conflict in the Strait of Hormuz was primarily caused by the lack of insurance for shipping companies.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。本文的核心在於報導美伊達成協議的同時，強調了恢復正常貿易的漫長道路，包括後勤挑戰（如地雷、船隻位置）、經濟風險（如儲備稀薄）以及不確定性（如核談判）。選項 A 錯誤，協議僅是框架，仍有 60 天談判期；選項 B 太過樂觀，與文中提到的「delicate state」不符；選項 D 錯誤，保險問題只是恢復過程中的眾多挑戰之一，並非衝突起因。
